pick.rationale: Lehigh's slight edge in efficiency metrics, particularly on offense, gives them the advantage in this First Four matchup. The Mountain Hawks come from a marginally stronger Patriot League and have shown resilience in winning their conference tournament. While Prairie View A&M is capable, Lehigh's better offensive ranking should be the difference in a closely contested game.

reasoningStep.title: Efficiency Analysis and Context Review
reasoningStep.summary: Lehigh holds a small but consistent advantage in efficiency ratings over Prairie View A&M, with better offensive metrics and a slightly higher overall ranking. Both teams are healthy and coming off conference tournament wins, but Lehigh's performance in a marginally tougher league supports their selection.
evidence: Torvik ranks: Lehigh 282, Prairie View A&M 292. KenPom adjusted efficiency margin: Lehigh -10.38, Prairie View A&M -11.1. Offensive ranking: Lehigh 290, Prairie View A&M 311. News articles confirm no significant injuries and first-ever meeting between the teams.

result.text: Ticket Punched: Previewing the Prairie View A&M Panthers For the 2026 NCAA Tournament Articles Podcasts Scout Team Game Analysis Bracketology Women's Hoops Free Content Team Previews Scoreboard Search Sign Up Sign Up Create Your Account Get access to exclusive features and stay updated. Log In Articles Podcasts Scout Team Game Analysis Bracketology Women's Hoops Free Content Team Previews Scoreboard Become a member Become a member Home Articles & Editorials Ticket Punched: Previewing the Prairie View A&M Panthers For the 2026 NCAA Tournament Articles & Editorials Articles & Editorials Bracketology Bracketology Free Content Free Content Ticket Punched: Previewing the Prairie View A&M Panthers For the 2026 NCAA Tournament Featured Mar 16, 2026 by 3MW_CBB The Panthers of Prairie View are back in March, snapping a 7-year hiatus from the NCAA Tournament. It wasn't so long ago head coach Byron Smith was feared as a perennial SWAC contender. Lately, however, this once proud program mysteriously got lost in the wilderness. Akin to his program resurrection back in 2019, Smith course corrected the Panthers again this year and maneuvered Prairie View to an improbable four-game run in the SWAC Championship. He leaned heavily on his ironman backcourt, Dontae Horn and Lance Williams, who clocked 155 of a possible 160 total minutes. Restoring a Lost Luster Bryon Smith was the toast of the SWAC before the COVID dark ages. He inherited one of the worst programs in the league in 2017. Five years later his Panthers were 66-19 in the SWAC, culminating in a flawless 13-0 campaign in 2020-21. A blowout loss in the 2021 SWAC Championship game to nearby rival Texas Southern would be the closest Smith would sniff the dance. From 2022 through 2025, this program was stuck in reverse. A combination of an eroding talent supply, failed JUCO recruits, and numerous injuries turned the Panthers back into a pumpkin. The difference this year is roste
